evals.prompt: Create a report showing the top 2 highest-paid employees in each department. Include their name, department, and salary. Sort departments alphabetically.
evals.expected_output: An Excel file in output/ with a timestamped name. Logo top-left, title beside it, summary row, then a data table with columns: Name, Department, Salary. 10 rows (2 per department × 5 departments), departments sorted A-Z, salary formatted as currency.

assertions.text: An .xlsx file was created in an output/ folder with a timestamp in the filename
The workbook has exactly 10 data rows (2 per department × 5 departments)
Departments appear in alphabetical order: Accounting, Marketing, Operation, Sales, Tech
Within each department, the 2 rows shown have the highest salaries for that department
The logo image is embedded in the workbook
A non-empty title appears beside the logo (not in column A)
A non-empty summary/description row appears below the logo area

evals.prompt: Generate an Excel report for the Tech and Sales departments only. Show all columns. Sort by salary descending within each department.
evals.expected_output: An Excel file with logo, title, summary, and a table showing employees from Tech and Sales only (20 rows). All columns included (name, department, salary). Within each department, rows sorted highest salary first. Salary formatted as currency.

assertions.text: An .xlsx file was created in an output/ folder with a timestamp in the filename
The workbook has exactly 20 data rows (10 Tech + 10 Sales)
No rows from Accounting, Marketing, or Operation departments are present
All source columns are present (name, department, salary)
Within each department, rows are sorted by salary from highest to lowest
The logo image is embedded in the workbook
A non-empty summary/description row appears below the logo area

evals.prompt: Create a department salary summary report. For each department, show the department name, number of employees, average salary, and the highest salary. Sort by average salary from highest to lowest.
evals.expected_output: An Excel file with logo, title, summary, and an aggregated table — one row per department (5 rows). Columns: Department, Employee Count, Average Salary, Highest Salary. Sorted so highest average salary is at the top. Salary columns formatted as currency.

assertions.text: An .xlsx file was created in an output/ folder with a timestamp in the filename
The workbook has exactly 5 data rows (one per department)
All 5 departments are represented: Accounting, Marketing, Operation, Sales, Tech
The table includes columns for employee count, average salary, and highest salary
Rows are sorted by average salary descending (Tech should appear first with ~$129,400 avg)
The logo image is embedded in the workbook
A non-empty summary/description row appears below the logo area
